questions.text: During POST, a computer produces a sequence of beeps before the display appears. What is the most accurate explanation?

options.text: The keyboard is confirming that Caps Lock is active
The BIOS/UEFI is communicating a hardware diagnostic status
The hard drive is formatting itself automatically
The operating system is loading its desktop environment

questions.answer: The BIOS/UEFI is communicating a hardware diagnostic status

questions.text: Which keyboard shortcut usually moves the cursor one word forward in a text editor?
questions.answer: Ctrl + Right Arrow

questions.text: In 3D printing, what is the main purpose of a slicer program?

options.text: It converts a 3D model into printable layer instructions
It increases the computer’s processor speed
It converts an image into a spreadsheet

questions.answer: It converts a 3D model into printable layer instructions

questions.text: Which of the following best describes a qubit in quantum computing?

options.text: A classical bit that can only store 0 or 1
A quantum unit of information that can exist in a superposition of states
A storage device used only in supercomputers
A file format used to save encrypted documents

questions.answer: A quantum unit of information that can exist in a superposition of states

questions.text: Which generation of computers is most closely associated with integrated circuits?
questions.answer: Third generation

questions.text: What is the full meaning of HDMI?
questions.answer: High-Definition Multimedia Interface

questions.text: Which of the following is the best example of system software?
questions.answer: Windows 11

questions.text: In Boolean logic, what is the output of an AND gate when the inputs are 1 and 0?
Which key is commonly used to toggle between insert mode and overwrite mode?
questions.answer: Insert

questions.text: What does CPU stand for?
questions.answer: Central Processing Unit

questions.text: Which of these best explains the function of BIOS/UEFI during startup?

options.text: It deletes temporary files before the desktop loads
It creates new user accounts before Windows starts
It automatically updates all installed applications
It checks and initializes hardware before handing control to the operating system

questions.answer: It checks and initializes hardware before handing control to the operating system

questions.text: Which logic gate produces an output of 1 when at least one input is 1?
questions.answer: OR

questions.text: What does Ctrl + Left Arrow usually do in a word processor or text editor?

options.text: Moves the cursor one word backward
Moves the cursor one character backward

questions.answer: Moves the cursor one word backward

questions.text: Which material is commonly used as filament in FDM 3D printing?
questions.answer: PLA

questions.text: Who is often called the father of the computer?
questions.answer: Charles Babbage

questions.text: Which of the following best describes POST?

options.text: A program used to design websites
A process that checks hardware when the computer is powered on
A system for printing documents automatically
A method of sending emails after booting

questions.answer: A process that checks hardware when the computer is powered on

questions.text: In quantum computing, what does superposition mean?

options.text: A qubit can represent a combination of states before measurement
A file exists in two folders at the same time
A processor is running two operating systems separately
A computer is connected to two monitors at once

questions.answer: A qubit can represent a combination of states before measurement

questions.text: Which shortcut is commonly used to save the current document?
questions.answer: Ctrl + S

questions.text: Which device commonly uses an RJ45 connector?
questions.answer: Ethernet network cable

questions.text: Which of these is an application software?
questions.answer: Microsoft Word

questions.text: What does RAM stand for?
questions.answer: Random Access Memory

questions.text: Which statement best describes a NOT gate?

options.text: It gives 1 only when both inputs are 1
It gives 1 when any input is 1

questions.answer: It reverses the input value

questions.text: What is the usual function of the Home key in a line of text?

options.text: Move the cursor to the end of the document
Open the browser home page in all programs
Move the cursor to the start of the line

questions.answer: Move the cursor to the start of the line

questions.text: Which computer generation used transistors?
questions.answer: Second generation

questions.text: Which file format is commonly used for 3D printable models?
questions.answer: STL

questions.text: Which of the following best defines an operating system?

options.text: Software that manages hardware resources and provides services to applications
A virus protection device attached to the motherboard
A hardware component that stores electricity
A cable used to connect a projector

questions.answer: Software that manages hardware resources and provides services to applications

questions.text: In quantum computing, what is quantum entanglement?

options.text: A technique for cooling a laptop fan
A method of compressing image files
A process of connecting many USB devices to one hub
A condition where quantum particles become linked so the state of one is related to another

questions.answer: A condition where quantum particles become linked so the state of one is related to another

questions.text: What is the main function of an HDMI cable?

options.text: To carry digital audio and video signals
To connect internal storage to the motherboard only

questions.answer: To carry digital audio and video signals

questions.text: Which shortcut is commonly used to undo the previous action?
questions.answer: Ctrl + Z

questions.text: Which of these is a utility program?

options.text: WordPad as a document editor only

questions.answer: Disk Cleanup

questions.text: What does ROM stand for?
questions.answer: Read Only Memory

questions.text: Which statement best describes the Page Down key?

options.text: It moves the document view one screen or page downward

questions.answer: It moves the document view one screen or page downward

questions.text: What is the output of an OR gate when inputs are 0 and 0?
What was ENIAC?

options.text: One of the earliest electronic general-purpose computers

questions.answer: One of the earliest electronic general-purpose computers

questions.text: Which of the following best describes a device driver?

options.text: A physical cable that powers the motherboard
A program that allows the operating system to communicate with hardware
A memory chip that stores the BIOS battery

questions.answer: A program that allows the operating system to communicate with hardware

questions.text: Which shortcut is commonly used to open the print dialog?
questions.answer: Ctrl + P

questions.text: Which of the following is a key difference between VGA and HDMI?

options.text: VGA usually carries analog video, while HDMI carries digital audio and video
VGA is digital while HDMI is analog
HDMI can only carry keyboard input
VGA is used only for internet connection

questions.answer: VGA usually carries analog video, while HDMI carries digital audio and video

questions.text: In 3D printing, what does the nozzle mainly do?
questions.answer: Melts and deposits filament material

questions.text: What does GUI stand for?
questions.answer: Graphical User Interface

questions.text: Which shortcut usually selects all content in a document or field?
questions.answer: Ctrl + A

questions.text: Which generation of computers used vacuum tubes?
questions.answer: First generation

questions.text: What is a truth table used for?

options.text: Measuring the speed of a graphics card
Listing all files on a hard drive
Showing the output of a logic circuit for all possible input combinations

questions.answer: Showing the output of a logic circuit for all possible input combinations

questions.text: What is the main purpose of antivirus software?

options.text: To convert HDMI to VGA automatically
To detect, prevent, and remove malicious software

questions.answer: To detect, prevent, and remove malicious software

questions.text: What does USB stand for?
questions.answer: Universal Serial Bus

questions.text: Which of these is most associated with quantum computing rather than classical computing?
questions.answer: Superposition

questions.text: Which shortcut is commonly used to cut selected text or an item?
questions.answer: Ctrl + X

questions.text: What is the function of the End key in many text editors?

options.text: Moves the cursor to the end of the line
Moves the cursor to the beginning of the line

questions.answer: Moves the cursor to the end of the line

questions.text: Which of the following is the best example of a special-purpose key?
questions.answer: Page Up

questions.text: Which of the following describes microprocessors in computer history?

options.text: They are the same as vacuum tubes
They are associated mainly with fourth-generation computers
They were first used in the abacus

questions.answer: They are associated mainly with fourth-generation computers

questions.text: What does URL stand for?
questions.answer: Uniform Resource Locator

questions.text: What does a NAND gate do?

options.text: Gives the same output as an OR gate
Gives the opposite output of an AND gate

questions.answer: Gives the opposite output of an AND gate

questions.text: Which statement best explains a special-purpose application?

options.text: Software that manages the entire computer system
Software designed to perform a specific task for a specific need

questions.answer: Software designed to perform a specific task for a specific need

questions.text: What is the purpose of the Page Up key?

options.text: Moves the view one screen or page upward
Moves the cursor one word backward

questions.answer: Moves the view one screen or page upward

questions.text: In 3D printing, what is infill?

options.text: The internal structure printed inside an object
The cable that connects the printer to HDMI
The outer colour of the monitor
The password used to protect a model file

questions.answer: The internal structure printed inside an object

questions.text: Which of these best explains Ctrl + V?

options.text: It pastes copied or cut content

questions.answer: It pastes copied or cut content

questions.text: What does LAN stand for?
questions.answer: Local Area Network

questions.text: What is the purpose of CMOS battery in many computers?

options.text: To help maintain BIOS/UEFI settings and system time when power is off
To connect the monitor to HDMI

questions.answer: To help maintain BIOS/UEFI settings and system time when power is off

questions.text: Which of these best describes a 3D model?

options.text: A digital representation of an object in three dimensions

questions.answer: A digital representation of an object in three dimensions

questions.text: Who is regarded as the first computer programmer?
questions.answer: Ada Lovelace

questions.text: Which shortcut is commonly used to redo an undone action?
questions.answer: Ctrl + Y

questions.text: Which statement about quantum measurement is most accurate?

options.text: Measuring a qubit always doubles computer memory
Measuring a qubit repairs damaged files
Measuring a qubit converts HDMI to VGA
Measuring a qubit can cause it to collapse to a definite classical state

questions.answer: Measuring a qubit can cause it to collapse to a definite classical state

questions.text: Which of these is an output device?
questions.answer: Monitor

questions.text: What is the full meaning of IP in networking?
questions.answer: Internet Protocol

questions.text: Which logic gate gives output 0 only when all inputs are 0?
questions.answer: OR

questions.text: What is one advantage of USB-C?

options.text: It replaces the need for an operating system
It is reversible and can support data, power, and display depending on the device
It is only used for analog video
It only works with floppy disks

questions.answer: It is reversible and can support data, power, and display depending on the device

questions.text: Which of these best describes a compiler?

options.text: A special key used to move up a page
A cable that transmits video signals
A program that translates source code into machine code or another lower-level form
A device that stores BIOS settings only

questions.answer: A program that translates source code into machine code or another lower-level form

questions.text: Which generation of computers is associated with artificial intelligence and knowledge-based systems?
questions.answer: Fifth generation

questions.text: What does Ctrl + C usually do?
questions.answer: Copies selected content

questions.text: Which of the following is a common function of a file manager?

options.text: Organizing, copying, moving, renaming, and deleting files

questions.answer: Organizing, copying, moving, renaming, and deleting files

questions.text: What is the output of a NOT gate if the input is 1?
Which of the following is a common purpose of Microsoft Excel?
questions.answer: Spreadsheet calculations and data analysis

questions.text: What is quantum parallelism often used to describe?

options.text: The process of opening several browser tabs
The arrangement of files in two columns
The ability of quantum systems to evaluate many possibilities through superposition
The connection of two keyboards to one computer

questions.answer: The ability of quantum systems to evaluate many possibilities through superposition

questions.text: Which of these is the most accurate description of boot order?

options.text: The order in which files are printed
The sequence of answers in a multiple-choice test
The order in which a computer checks devices for a bootable operating system
The arrangement of keyboard keys alphabetically

questions.answer: The order in which a computer checks devices for a bootable operating system

questions.text: Which part of a 3D printer is usually heated to melt filament?
questions.answer: Hot end/nozzle assembly

questions.text: What does ICT stand for?
questions.answer: Information and Communication Technology

questions.text: What is the difference between BIOS and POST?
questions.answer: BIOS/UEFI is firmware that initializes hardware and starts the boot process, while POST is the diagnostic test performed during startup to check whether essential hardware is working.

questions.text: What does Ctrl + Left Arrow do in most text editors?
questions.answer: It moves the cursor one word backward.

questions.text: State two examples of system software.
questions.answer: Windows and Linux.

questions.text: What is the purpose of a device driver?
questions.answer: A device driver allows the operating system to communicate correctly with a hardware device.

questions.text: What is the full meaning of HDMI?
questions.answer: High-Definition Multimedia Interface.

questions.text: State one advantage of HDMI over VGA.
questions.answer: HDMI carries digital audio and video, while VGA usually carries analog video only.

questions.text: What is slicing in 3D printing?
questions.answer: Slicing is the process of converting a 3D model into layers and machine instructions that a 3D printer can follow.

questions.text: What is infill in 3D printing?
questions.answer: Infill is the internal printed structure inside a 3D object that affects its strength, weight, and material usage.

questions.text: Who designed the Analytical Engine?
questions.answer: Charles Babbage.

questions.text: Why is Ada Lovelace important in computer history?
questions.answer: Ada Lovelace is regarded as the first computer programmer because she wrote an algorithm intended for Charles Babbage’s Analytical Engine.

questions.text: What does the Insert key usually do?
questions.answer: It toggles between insert mode and overwrite mode.

questions.text: What does the Home key usually do in text editing?
questions.answer: It moves the cursor to the beginning of the current line.

questions.text: What does the Page Up key do?
questions.answer: It moves the document or screen view one page or screen upward.

questions.text: What is the function of an AND gate?
questions.answer: An AND gate gives an output of 1 only when all its inputs are 1.

questions.text: What is the function of a NOT gate?
questions.answer: A NOT gate reverses the input value; 1 becomes 0 and 0 becomes 1.

questions.text: What is a truth table?
questions.answer: A truth table shows all possible input combinations of a logic circuit and the output produced for each combination.

questions.text: What is a qubit?
questions.answer: A qubit is the basic unit of quantum information and can exist in a superposition of 0 and 1 before measurement.

questions.text: What is superposition in quantum computing?
questions.answer: Superposition is the ability of a qubit to represent a combination of possible states before it is measured.

questions.text: What is quantum entanglement?
questions.answer: Quantum entanglement is a link between quantum particles where the state of one particle is connected to the state of another, even when separated.

questions.text: What happens when a qubit is measured?
questions.answer: When a qubit is measured, it collapses from a superposition into a definite classical state such as 0 or 1.

questions.text: What does POST stand for?
questions.answer: Power-On Self-Test.

questions.text: What is the purpose of the CMOS battery?
questions.answer: It helps maintain BIOS/UEFI settings and the system clock when the computer is powered off.

questions.text: What is the full meaning of GUI?
questions.answer: Graphical User Interface.

questions.text: What is the purpose of antivirus software?
questions.answer: Antivirus software detects, prevents, quarantines, and removes malicious software.

questions.text: What is the difference between application software and system software?
questions.answer: System software manages the computer’s hardware and provides a platform for other programs, while application software helps users perform specific tasks such as typing, browsing, designing, or calculating.
